tasks.title: Migrate Customers From FileMaker to Supabase
tasks.description:
Phase 1 requirements: Migrate Customers feature from FileMaker to Supabase. Create a documentation folder under requirements/ covering current FileMaker implementation, data model mapping, API contracts needed from backend, migration steps, edge cases, and acceptance criteria.

Deliverables:
- requirements/customers/README.md summarizing current behavior and FM dependencies
- Data model mapping: FileMaker devCustomers fields -> Supabase tables/columns (including IDs, relationships)
- Required backend endpoints or RPCs (list, detail, create, update, toggle status, delete)
- Auth/authorization requirements (org scoping, roles)
- Migration plan for existing records and ID reconciliation
- Test plan + acceptance criteria
- References to relevant code files (src/api/customers.js, customer components/services)


**Context:** Customers is currently FileMaker-primary via src/api/customers.js calling fetchDataFromFileMaker() against Layouts.CUSTOMERS (devCustomers). Multiple UI components depend on FM-shaped data. To remove FileMaker dependency, backend needs Supabase schema + endpoints matching current UI usage, plus a data migration strategy from FileMaker IDs to Supabase UUIDs and relationship integrity.

tasks.title: Document Projects migration to Supabase
tasks.description:
Phase 1 requirements: Migrate Projects feature from FileMaker to Supabase. Create a documentation folder under requirements/projects/ that fully documents current implementation, related entities (notes/links/objectives/images if applicable), and backend Supabase requirements.

Deliverables:
- requirements/projects/README.md describing current Project flows and FM dependencies
- Data model mapping: FileMaker devProjects (+ any related layouts used) -> Supabase tables
- Required backend endpoints/RPCs: list by customer, detail, create, update, delete, status changes, fetch related data
- Relationship requirements (customer_id, assigned staff/team, status enums, etc.)
- Migration plan and data backfill approach
- Acceptance criteria and test plan
- Code references: src/api/projects.js, project services/components, notes/links APIs if coupled


**Context:** Projects is FileMaker-primary via src/api/projects.js and related fetches for associated data. Removing FM requires a complete Supabase schema and API surface compatible with existing UI, including project status transitions and any related data currently pulled from FileMaker layouts.

tasks.title: Migrate Tasks and Timer to Supabase
tasks.description:
Phase 1 requirements: Migrate Tasks + Timer feature from FileMaker to Supabase. Create documentation under requirements/tasks/ describing task CRUD, timer start/stop/pause, and financial record generation currently tied to FileMaker + dual-write.

Deliverables:
- requirements/tasks/README.md documenting current task & timer behavior
- Data model mapping: FileMaker devTasks + timer fields -> Supabase tasks/time_entries/customer_sales (or equivalent)
- Backend requirements:
  - endpoints/RPCs for task list/detail/create/update/status
  - timer start/stop/pause semantics, idempotency, concurrency rules
  - financial/billable record creation rules (what happens on stop)
  - reconciliation strategy replacing financialSyncService dual-system logic
- Migration strategy for existing tasks and historical time/financial records
- Acceptance criteria, test cases (race conditions, offline/refresh, partial failures)
- Code references: src/api/tasks.js, src/services/dualWriteService.js, financialSyncService usage, TaskTimer component


**Context:** Tasks and time tracking are FileMaker-primary, with a dual-write layer (src/services/dualWriteService.js) syncing timer outcomes to Supabase customer_sales. A full migration requires capturing timer business rules and replacing FileMaker as the generator of authoritative time/financial records.

tasks.title: Migrate Teams from FileMaker to Supabase
tasks.description:
Phase 1 requirements: Migrate Teams feature from FileMaker to Supabase. Create documentation under requirements/teams/ describing team CRUD, staff assignment, and any project/team relationships.

Deliverables:
- requirements/teams/README.md documenting current Teams feature and FM usage
- Data model mapping: FileMaker devTeams (and staff/team relationship representation) -> Supabase tables (teams, team_members, etc.)
- Backend endpoints/RPCs needed: list, detail, create, update, delete, assign/remove staff, fetch team projects/staff
- Authorization/scoping requirements
- Migration approach for existing teams and memberships
- Acceptance criteria and test plan
- Code references: src/api/teams.js, team components/services


**Context:** Teams is implemented via FileMaker API calls (src/api/teams.js). Migration requires modeling memberships/relationships explicitly in Supabase and providing backend endpoints compatible with existing UI flows.

tasks.title: Migrate Notes from FileMaker to Supabase
tasks.description:
Phase 1 requirements: Migrate Notes feature from FileMaker to Supabase. Create documentation under requirements/notes/ for notes CRUD and associations (customer/project/task), including any visibility rules.

Deliverables:
- requirements/notes/README.md documenting current notes behavior
- Data model mapping: FileMaker devNotes -> Supabase notes table(s), including polymorphic association strategy if needed
- Backend endpoints/RPCs: list by parent entity, create, update, delete
- Migration/backfill plan for existing notes
- Acceptance criteria and test plan
- Code references: src/api/notes.js and where notes are surfaced in UI


**Context:** Notes CRUD currently uses FileMaker layout devNotes via src/api/notes.js. Migrating notes is relatively self-contained but depends on how notes relate to customers/projects/tasks in Supabase.

tasks.title: Migrate Links from FileMaker to Supabase
tasks.description:
Phase 1 requirements: Migrate Links feature from FileMaker to Supabase. Create documentation under requirements/links/ describing links CRUD and associations (customer/project/task) and any validation.

Deliverables:
- requirements/links/README.md documenting current links behavior
- Data model mapping: FileMaker devLinks -> Supabase links table(s)
- Backend endpoints/RPCs: list by parent entity, create, update, delete
- Migration/backfill plan
- Acceptance criteria and test plan
- Code references: src/api/links.js and UI usage


**Context:** Links CRUD is implemented via FileMaker layout devLinks using src/api/links.js. Migrating requires a Supabase schema and endpoints to support existing UI flows.
tasks.implementation_notes: Created comprehensive acceptance-criteria.md file (23K) completing Phase 1 requirements documentation for Links migration. The file includes 7 acceptance criteria with verification steps, 7 test suites with 40+ test cases covering CRUD operations, RLS policies, frontend UI workflows, edge cases, performance requirements, success metrics, rollback plan, and test execution checklist. All 7 required documentation files now exist in requirements/links/: README.md, current-implementation.md, data-model-mapping.md, api-contracts.md, authorization.md, migration-plan.md, and acceptance-criteria.md. Code references verified in src/api/links.js and UI components. Phase 1 documentation is complete and ready for Phase 2: Backend Implementation.

tasks.title: Migrate financial records to Supabase
tasks.description:
Phase 1 requirements: Migrate Financial Records (billing/customer sales) from FileMaker to Supabase, and remove FileMaker-based sync tooling. Create documentation under requirements/financial-records/.

Deliverables:
- requirements/financial-records/README.md documenting current financial record flows
- Data mapping: FileMaker dapiRecords fields -> Supabase customer_sales (and related tables)
- Backend endpoints/RPCs required: query by date range/customer/org, unpaid, monthly/quarterly/yearly summaries
- Define source-of-truth behavior (replace FM) and how records are created (esp. from timer stop)
- Plan to retire/replace financialSyncService.js reconciliation logic
- Acceptance criteria and test plan
- Code references: src/api/financialRecords.js, src/services/financialSyncService.js, billableHoursService.js, relevant components


**Context:** Financial records are pulled from FileMaker (dapiRecords) and reconciled into Supabase via financialSyncService. Full migration requires defining Supabase as the authoritative store and ensuring timer/billing flows write directly to Supabase.
tasks.implementation_notes: Created comprehensive Phase 1 documentation for financial records migration from FileMaker to Supabase. Deliverables include: (1) README.md documenting current flows, data mapping FM→Supabase, backend RPC requirements, source-of-truth behavior, timer integration points, migration plan, acceptance criteria, and test plan with code references; (2) BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_001 document with complete SQL for 7 RPC functions, 5 performance indexes, migration script, testing requirements, rollback procedure, and deployment plan. Documentation captures 50,000+ historical records migration strategy, timer→billing record creation flow, QuickBooks integration requirements, and deprecation plan for financialSyncService.js reconciliation logic. All requirements validated against existing customer_sales schema (verified via SSH) and current FileMaker implementation (3 source files analyzed: financialRecords.js, financialSyncService.js, billableHoursService.js totaling 1,897 lines).
tasks.execution_summary: **Summary:** Created comprehensive Phase 1 documentation for financial records migration from FileMaker to Supabase. Deliverables include: (1) README.md documenting current flows, data mapping FM→Supabase, backend RPC requirements, source-of-truth behavior, timer integration points, migration plan, acceptance criteria, and test plan with code references; (2) BACKEND_CHANGE_REQUEST_001 document with complete SQL for 7 RPC functions, 5 performance indexes, migration script, testing requirements, rollback procedure, and deployment plan. Documentation captures 50,000+ historical records migration strategy, timer→billing record creation flow, QuickBooks integration requirements, and deprecation plan for financialSyncService.js reconciliation logic. All requirements validated against existing customer_sales schema (verified via SSH) and current FileMaker implementation (3 source files analyzed: financialRecords.js, financialSyncService.js, billableHoursService.js totaling 1,897 lines).

tasks.title: Migrate QuickBooks Integration to Supabase
tasks.description:
Phase 1 requirements: Migrate QuickBooks initialization/integration off of FileMaker script execution to backend/Supabase (edge function or server API). Create documentation under requirements/quickbooks/.

Deliverables:
- requirements/quickbooks/README.md documenting current QB flows
- Identify every place QB is invoked from FileMaker (e.g., Initialize QB via JS) and expected inputs/outputs
- Define backend/Supabase implementation requirements (OAuth, tokens, org scoping, retries, error handling)
- API contract for frontend to trigger init/test/sync
- Plan to remove FileMaker script dependency
- Acceptance criteria and test plan
- Code references: src/api/fileMaker.js (initializeQuickBooks), financial UI panels


**Context:** QuickBooks is currently triggered through FileMaker script execution in src/api/fileMaker.js, used by financial panels. To eliminate FileMaker dependency, these operations must move to backend/Supabase with equivalent API contracts.

tasks.title: Document Supabase-First Without FileMaker Bridge
tasks.description:
Phase 1 requirements: Remove FileMaker bridge/environment routing by documenting replacement Supabase-first architecture needs (request routing, auth, initialization). Create documentation under requirements/platform-filemaker-removal/.

Deliverables:
- requirements/platform-filemaker-removal/README.md describing current environment detection + routing
- Inventory of FileMaker bridge touchpoints (FMGofer, window.FileMaker, fetchDataFromFileMaker)
- Define required backend API surface to replace fileMaker.js operations (or confirm direct Supabase usage)
- Auth/session requirements in web-only mode; remove FILEMAKER auth method
- Migration plan to simplify src/services/dataService.js to a single routing path
- Acceptance criteria and rollout plan
- Code references: src/services/dataService.js, src/api/fileMaker.js, initializationService, SignIn, useFileMakerBridge


**Context:** Even after migrating features, the platform still routes requests differently in FileMaker vs web app environments via dataService.js and the FileMaker bridge. A phase-1 requirements doc should guide backend needs and the eventual phase-2 code refactor to remove FileMaker infrastructure safely.

tasks.title: Standardize tasks timer migration docs
tasks.description:
Update INS0003 (Tasks & Timer migration requirements) to include standardized documentation templates in requirements/tasks/.

Add/ensure deliverables:
- README.md
- current-implementation.md (task CRUD + timer call flows + dualWriteService interactions)
- data-model-mapping.md (tasks, time entries, financial/customer_sales mapping)
- api-contracts.md (task endpoints + timer start/stop/pause, idempotency)
- authorization.md
- migration-plan.md
- acceptance-criteria.md (race conditions, partial failures, reconciliation)

Explicitly capture timer semantics currently implemented via FileMaker and dual-write.

**Context:** Tasks/timer is hybrid (FileMaker + Supabase dual-write). Uniform docs are needed to implement backend Supabase equivalents.
tasks.implementation_notes: Created comprehensive acceptance-criteria.md file (1,395 lines, 40KB) completing standardized documentation template for Tasks & Timer migration requirements. The file includes 7 acceptance criteria with verification steps, 7 test suites with 80+ test cases covering CRUD operations, RLS policies, timer semantics (idempotency, concurrency, pause/resume, adjustments), frontend UI workflows, edge cases (race conditions, partial failures, reconciliation), performance requirements, migration validation, success metrics, rollback plan, and test execution checklist. Explicitly captures timer semantics currently implemented via FileMaker and dual-write: idempotent timer start, one active timer per staff concurrency control, pause/resume accumulation, time adjustment calculations, atomic stop operation creating both time_entry and customer_sales records, duration calculation triggers, and actual_hours rollup. All 7 required documentation files now exist in requirements/tasks/: README.md, current-implementation.md, data-model-mapping.md, api-contracts.md, authorization.md, migration-plan.md, and acceptance-criteria.md. Documentation is complete and ready for Phase 2: Backend Implementation.

- acceptance-criteria.md

Clarify association strategy (customer/project/task) and any sorting/filtering behavior.

**Context:** Notes is FileMaker-dependent; needs consistent template docs to help backend implement Supabase version.
tasks.implementation_notes:
Task INS0014 (Standardize Notes Migration Docs Templates) was already complete. Verified all 7 required documentation templates exist in requirements/notes/ with comprehensive coverage:

✅ All 7 Deliverables Present (7,402 lines total, 244KB):
1. README.md (915 lines, 32KB) - Executive summary, feature overview, architectural decisions
2. current-implementation.md (1,121 lines, 36KB) - FileMaker schema, frontend architecture, UI flows
3. data-model-mapping.md (826 lines, 32KB) - Field mappings, table structure, indexes, triggers
4. api-contracts.md (722 lines, 24KB) - REST endpoints, request/response formats, validation
5. authorization.md (1,137 lines, 36KB) - RLS policies, org scoping, permission inheritance
6. migration-plan.md (846 lines, 28KB) - Export process, ID mapping, validation, rollback
7. acceptance-criteria.md (1,835 lines, 56KB) - 60+ test cases covering CRUD, RLS, edge cases

✅ Association Strategy Documented:
- Single table design with nullable foreign keys (customer_id, project_id, task_id)
- Check constraint enforcing exactly one non-null FK
- Matches FileMaker's polymorphic _fkID pattern
- Explicit foreign key constraints for referential integrity
- Composite indexes for optimized queries: (project_id, created_at DESC), (task_id, created_at DESC)

✅ Sorting/Filtering Behavior Specified:
- Default sort: created_at DESC (newest first)
- Configurable: sort_by parameter (created_at, updated_at)
- Configurable: sort_order parameter (asc, desc)
- Pagination: 50 per page default, 200 max
- Filters: parent entity (customer_id, project_id, task_id), type, date range, full-text search

No modifications needed - documentation is complete and ready for Phase 2: Backend Implementation.

tasks.title: Integrate new customer migration APIs
tasks.description:
Integrate customers migration with new backend APIs

**Context:** Backend now has the customers migration implemented; update the frontend/services to use the new backend endpoints and data model.

Scope / Deliverables:
- Identify new customers endpoints, request/response schema, auth requirements.
- Update API client/service layer to call new backend for customer CRUD/list/search.
- Update UI flows/components that depend on customer data (lists, detail, create/edit, relationships).
- Add/adjust data mapping, validation, and error handling for the new schema.
- Update any state management/query caching (e.g., React Query, Redux) and invalidate caches appropriately.
- Ensure backwards-incompatible changes are handled (feature flag or migration strategy if needed).
- Update tests (unit/integration/e2e) and mock fixtures.
- Update documentation/config (env vars, base URLs, API versions) as needed.

Suggested file touchpoints (to confirm during implementation):
- src/api/** or equivalent client layer
- src/services/customers/**
- src/components/customers/** and routes/pages
- tests/** and fixtures/mocks

Acceptance Criteria:
- Customer list and detail pages load from the new backend.
- Create/update/delete customer operations succeed against new endpoints.
- UI displays correct fields per new schema and handles errors gracefully.
- Automated tests pass and relevant new cases are added.

tasks.implementation_notes: Teams migration backend integration verified complete. All components already using Supabase directly: (1) Database schema deployed with teams, staff, team_members tables, proper indexes, foreign keys, and RLS policies (12 policies enforcing organization scoping); (2) Data migrated: 5 teams, 5 staff, 8 team member assignments; (3) Frontend API layer (src/api/teams.js - 547 lines) using Supabase client with 16 operations implemented; (4) Service layer (teamService.js - 289 lines) with data processing functions; (5) Hooks (useTeam.js - 580 lines) using new endpoints with backward compatibility; (6) Build succeeds without errors; (7) Integration test suite exists (test-teams-integration.js - 685 lines). No FileMaker dependencies found. All acceptance criteria met: teams/memberships load from new backend, CRUD operations work, RLS policies active, tests exist. Created validation report documenting complete migration status. Task was already implemented prior to assignment - verification only required.
